text.title: Upselling and Cross-Selling Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-upselling-cross-selling` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Trigger upsell offers from usage signals (80% of limit, advanced-feature use, team growth) NOT from calendar dates. Calendar-based upsell blasts to non-triggered accounts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Calendar-based blasts produce low conversion and high opt-out. Trigger-based offers reach accounts when the value proposition is visibly true to them.

rule.statement: Frame the upsell message with the customer's own usage data point (e.g. '900 of 1,000 subscribers'). Generic upgrade prompts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Personal-data anchoring converts 3-5x better than generic prompts because the value is observably true to the recipient.

rule.statement: Upgrade UX MUST support one-click upgrade with prorated billing. Forcing the customer through a sales conversation or full re-checkout is forbidden for tier-to-tier expansion.
rule.rationale: Friction kills expansion conversion. One-click + proration removes the dominant abandonment cause (manual sales motion or surprise charge).

rule.statement: Report Net Revenue Retention (NRR) and expansion ARR monthly with the upsell-conversion funnel (trigger → offer → click → upgrade). Aggregating expansion into 'revenue' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hiding expansion in headline revenue obscures whether retention or expansion is driving growth. Disaggregated reporting forces focus.

rule.statement: Agents and automated systems may NOT issue discounts, credits, or downgrades autonomously. These require named-human approval per the human-checkpoint policy.
rule.rationale: Autonomous discounts erode margin and create policy inconsistency across customers. Human approval is required for any revenue-affecting concession.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
